milestones.title: Wire the AHE Loop
milestones.vision: Wire AHE's existing infrastructure into a working feedback loop. The startup hook, skill files, session manifests, and pipeline all exist but are disconnected — no hook fires, findings are invisible, sessions aren't captured. This milestone connects them so AHE is demonstrably active on every session.
success_criteria: User opens Qwen Code and sees a daily health forecast automatically
User sees pipeline findings without checking log files
Session manifests are captured for every session (not just 3 in 5 days)
AHE startup hook does not break existing hooks (mcp-startup-cleanup, rtk-wrapper)

key_risks.risk: Qwen Code hook execution environment differs from user shell
key_risks.whyItMatters: Hooks run in a Node.js context under Qwen Code's process. File system access and child_process are available but environment variables, PATH, and working directory may differ from an interactive PowerShell session. This broke the reseed script reference before.
Fires before every tool call. If heartbeat write is slow, tool calls are delayed. Must be lightweight (<5ms per invocation).

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Startup hook executes without errors in real Qwen Code session, using only Node.js built-ins
Heartbeat write completes in under 5ms using fs.writeFileSync with <1KB payload

milestones.verification_contract:
- Hook scripts run standalone without syntax errors (node --check)
- Hook registration in settings.json is valid JSON (node -e JSON.parse)
- Health report output contains expected sections (Status, Sessions, Benchmark, System Health)
- Pipeline-findings.json valid when reseed script runs
milestones.verification_integration:
- Startup hook output appears in Qwen Code conversation context
- AHEDailyBrief scheduled task runs without PowerShell errors
- Session heartbeat visible in ~/.ahe/status/ after tool call
- Pipeline findings section appears in health report when data available
milestones.verification_operational:
- Works across Qwen Code restarts
- Handles back-to-back sessions within same day (only fires once)
- Survives machine reboot (scheduled tasks fire on schedule)
- Handles missing pipeline data gracefully
milestones.verification_uat:
- User opens Qwen Code: health report appears
- User runs /ahe-daily: brief generates
- User ends session: manifest written
definition_of_done: Startup hook fires and injects health report on first daily session
Pipeline findings surfaced in health report
Session manifests captured reliably (heartbeat + stale detection)
QWEN.md AHE section reflects working state
milestones.requirement_coverage:
Covers: R001 (S01), R002 (S02), R003 (S03), R004 (S01), R005 (S04)
Leaves for later: differential measurement, skill pruning, auto-QWEN.md updates

# S01: Wire the Startup Hook

**Registered startup hook, created reseed bridge, fixed scheduled task**

## What Happened

Registered ahe-startup-check.js in settings.json SessionStart hooks. Created ahe-reseed-daily.ps1 bridging pipeline outputs to AHE status. Fixed AHEDailyBrief scheduled task. All verified: hook fires, reseed runs, task exits 0.

## Verification

Hook registered. Reseed runs. Task exits 0. Full health report generates. All 3 tasks completed.

tasks.description: Update AHE section to document 4 services, registered hooks, heartbeat-backed closure, pipeline findings bridge

decisions.decision: AHE lifecycle integration with Qwen Code
decisions.choice: Use Qwen Code's native hooks system (SessionStart, PreToolUse) rather than MCP or external daemon
decisions.rationale: Research of OMX (28k stars), Oh My Pi, GSD2, and Hermes Agent shows that meta-layers integrate via lifecycle hooks + config injection, not MCP. MCP is for external tool exposure. Qwen Code already supports SessionStart and PreToolUse hook types in settings.json. This matches the industry pattern.

decisions.choice: Two-layer: startup hook surfaces findings in conversation context (immediate), reseed script persists pipeline findings to status file (durable)
decisions.rationale: Layer 1 gives immediate visibility with no risk (startup hook already exists). Layer 2 provides persistence across sessions. Avoids auto-editing QWEN.md until differential measurement proves value.

decisions.choice: PreToolUse heartbeat hook tracks ongoing state; stale-detection on next startup infers ended sessions; QWEN.md instruction triggers closure in-session
decisions.rationale: Qwen Code has no native SessionEnd hook type. PreToolUse provides the only reliable hook point. Writing cumulative state per-tool-call is lightweight and ensures the last session's state is never lost. Stale detection handles abrupt exits.
